statement.p: An artist sells his paintings at {\$17.00} per piece. Currently, he can sell 110 paintings per year. So his annual income from paintings is 17\cdot110=1870 dollars. He plans to raise the price. However, for each {\$4.00} of price increase per painting, his customers would buy 9 fewer paintings annually.
Assume the artist would raise the price of his painting x times, each time by {\$4.00}. Use an expanded polynomial to represent his new income per year.

solution.p: Currently, each painting costs {\$17.00}. After raising the price x times, each time by {\$4.00}, each paintings new price would be {17+4x} dollars.
Currently, the artist sells 110 paintings per year. After raising the price x times, each time selling 9 fewer paintings, he would sell {110-9x} paintings per year.
His annual income can be calculated by multiplying each paintings price with the number of paintings he would sell:
After raising the price x times, each time by {\$4.00}, the artists annual income from paintings would be {-36x^{2}+287x+1870} dollars.
